name: GitHub Release to Audio Newsletter — ChatGPT AI Script + OpenAI TTS + Drive + Sheets + Gmail

parameters.content:
## GitHub Release to Audio Newsletter — ChatGPT AI Script + OpenAI TTS + Google Drive + Sheets + Gmail

For product teams who want every GitHub release announced as a podcast-style audio newsletter — no manual recording. When you publish a new GitHub Release, this workflow converts the technical changelog into a warm spoken script with a ChatGPT AI model, turns it into an MP3 using OpenAI text-to-speech, hosts it on Google Drive, and emails an HTML newsletter with a listen link to your subscriber list.

## How it works
- **1. GitHub — On New Release Published** fires when a release is published
- **2. Code — Parse Release Changelog** cleans the markdown and categorizes features, fixes, and improvements
- **3. AI Agent — Write Spoken Script** uses a ChatGPT AI model to write a 60-90 second spoken script
- **OpenAI Chat Model** the language model attached to the AI Agent
- **4. Code — Extract Script Text** pulls the clean script and carries the release data forward
- **5. HTTP — OpenAI TTS Generate MP3** converts the script to an MP3 voice file
- **6. Google Drive — Save MP3 File** uploads the audio to your Drive folder
- **7. Google Drive — Make File Public** sets the file to anyone-with-link so subscribers can play it
- **8. Code — Store Drive URL and Release Data** builds the public listen URL
- **9. Google Sheets — Load Subscribers** reads your subscriber list
- **10. Aggregate — Bundle All Subscriber Emails** collects all emails into one item
- **11. Code — Build HTML Newsletter** builds the BCC list and the HTML email
- **12. Gmail — Send Audio Newsletter** sends the newsletter via BCC

## Set up steps
1. **GitHub** — connect a GitHub credential (Personal Access Token with repo read), replace `YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME` and `YOUR_REPO_NAME` in node 1. n8n creates the webhook automatically when you activate
2. **OpenAI** — connect one OpenAI API credential and use it in both **OpenAI Chat Model** (pick any current ChatGPT model) and node 5 (TTS). TTS bills per character — a typical release script costs roughly one US cent
3. **Google Drive** — create a folder for the audio, copy its folder ID from the URL after /folders/, replace `YOUR_AUDIO_FOLDER_ID` in node 6, and connect Google Drive OAuth2
4. **Google Sheets** — create a sheet with a tab named Subscribers and an Email column, add subscriber emails one per row, replace `YOUR_SUBSCRIBERS_SHEET_ID` in node 9, and connect Google Sheets OAuth2
5. **Gmail** — connect Gmail OAuth2 and replace `YOUR_SENDER_EMAIL` in node 12. The BCC list is built automatically from the subscriber sheet

## GitHub Trigger and Parse
Webhook fires when a release is published. Code cleans the markdown changelog and extracts features, fixes, and improvements into categories.

## AI Script and Text-to-Speech
The ChatGPT AI model writes a 60-90 second spoken script. OpenAI text-to-speech then generates the MP3 voice file with the alloy voice.

nodes.name: Section — Host, Load Subscribers, and Email

parameters.content:
## Host, Load Subscribers, and Email
The MP3 is saved to Drive and made public. Subscribers are loaded from Sheets and bundled. The HTML newsletter is built and sent via Gmail BCC.

options.systemMessage:
You are a professional tech podcast host. Convert the provided GitHub release changelog into a warm, engaging spoken audio script.

The script will be read aloud by a TTS voice, so:
- Write in natural spoken English — no markdown, no bullet points, no headers, no special characters
- Avoid jargon and technical abbreviations unless they are universally known
- Use conversational transitions like 'First up', 'Also worth mentioning', 'And finally'
- Keep it between 60 and 90 seconds when read aloud (approximately 150-225 words)
- Start with a warm greeting mentioning the version and date
- Briefly mention the most important 2-3 changes in natural language
- End with a call to action to check the full release notes
- Tone: professional but friendly, like an internal company podcast

Return ONLY the spoken script text — nothing else, no labels, no preamble.

nodes.name: 8. Code — Store Drive URL and Release Data
